LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EXXONMOBIL OIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED AS TO OBJECTIONS 4, 13, 19, 23, 24, 25, 28 AND 29; OVERRULED AS TO NUMBER 33; NUMBER 44 IS SUSTAINED IN PART. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR 	581 - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT EXXONMOBIL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART. BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAVE PLED CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONTINUING NUISANCE AND TRESPASS ONLY, PLAINTIFF IS LIMITED TO DAMAGES FOR THE THREE YEARS PROCEEDING THIS SUIT. PLAINTIFF'S PAST DAMAGES BEFORE 6/29/06 AND PROSPECTIVE DAMAGES ARE BARRED AS A MATTER OF LAW. MANGINI V.AEROJET-GENERAL CORP. (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 1087, 1093; BECK DEV. CO. V. SO.PAC. TRANSP. CO. (1996) 44 CAL.APP.4TH 1160, 1216-17. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE GROUND THAT THE ALLEGED DAMAGES ARE TOO SPECULATIVE IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE ALLEGED DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF ACCESS TO WHARF J-10 IS BARRED BY COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL IS DENIED. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURTS RULING BY TELEPHONE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. THE COURT RULED ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS AS FOLLOWS: OBJECTION NUMBER 16 IS SUSTAINED, OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINDER. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED AS TO NUMBERS 1, 3 AND 4; NUMBER 5 IS OVERRULED IN PART; REMAINDER OF THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR 	581 - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CCSF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. AS TO THE RES JUDICATA/COLLATERAL ESPTOPPEL ARGUMENTS ON THE 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THERE WAS NO REFERENCE IN THE PRIOR ACTION TO "PERMANENT" LOSS OF ACCESS. THE ISSUES IN THE COURT OF APPEAL DECISION INVOLVED THE DUTY TO INSPECT AND REPAIR UNDER THE LEASE. BY IMPLICATION, A DUTY TO REPAIR MEANS THAT THE WHARF MUST BE REPAIRED AND, THEREFORE, LOSS OF ACCESS WOULD NOT BE PERMANENT. FURTHERMORE, THE JURY AWARDED PLAINTIFF $2.8 MILLION FOR CONSTRUCTION COSTS TO REBUILD THE PROCESSING FACILITY, ALSO IMPLYING THAT THE CASE WAS NOT ABOUT A PERMANENT LOSS OF ACCESS. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, PLAINTIFF HAS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO SEVERAL ALLEGED OBLIGATIONS AND BREACHES. AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF QUIET ENJOYMENT, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF THE COVENANT COULD NOT BE ESTABLISHED. THERE IS PLENTY OF EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT (PORT NAMED AS DISCHARGERS OF THE POLLUTION INITIALLY AND LATER IN TIME WHEN IT DEMOLISHED THE WHARF; THE POLLUTION HAS PREVENTED PLAINTIFF FROM REBUILDING ITS FACILITIES.) PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES P. WALSH & ATTY. GWEN L. FANGER OF DAVIS WRIGHT TREMAINE LLP, TEL. NO. 276-6556 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DONALD P. MARGOLIS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3853 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION OF THE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cognizable claim for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing in light of express provision in the lease that the Port has no obligation to maintain the substructure or the leased premises. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =302/HK

Off Calendar Aug-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF F. ALIOTO COMPANY, INC'S MOTION TO SEAL EXHIBIT G TO THE DECLARATION OF LAURA MCKASKLE.
Appearances: James P. Walsh and Gwen L. Fanger of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (276.6556) for plaintiff F. Alioto Company, Inc; Donald P. Margolis of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3853) for defendant City and County of San Francisco; Laura McKaskle and Michael Molland of Morgan Lewis (213.612.7281) for defendant ExxonMobil Oil Corporation.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EXXONMOBIL OIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: James P. Walsh and Gwen L. Fanger of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (276.6556) for plaintiff F. Alioto Company, Inc; Donald P. Margolis of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3853) for defendant City and County of San Francisco; Laura McKaskle and Michael Molland of Morgan Lewis (213.612.7281) for defendant ExxonMobil Oil Corporation.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's evidence are overruled. Defendant's objections are sustained as to objections 4, 13, 19, 23, 24, 25, 28 and 29; overruled as to number 33; number 44 is sustained in part. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR 581
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: Defendant ExxonMobil's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is granted in part. Because Plaintiffs have pled causes of action for continuing nuisance and trespass only, Plaintiff is limited to damages for the three years proceeding this suit. Plaintiff's past damages before 6/29/06 and prospective damages are barred as a matter of law. Mangini v.Aerojet-General Corp. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 1087, 1093; Beck Dev. Co. v. So.Pac. Transp. Co. (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1160, 1216-17.
The Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication on the ground that the alleged damages are too speculative is denied.
The Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication on the grounds that the alleged damages for loss of access to Wharf J-10 is barred by collateral estoppel is denied. Parties notified of the courts ruling by telephone. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: James P. Walsh and Gwen L. Fanger of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (276.6556) for plaintiff F. Alioto Company, Inc; Donald P. Margolis of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3853) for defendant City and County of San Francisco; Laura McKaskle and Michael Molland of Morgan Lewis (213.612.7281) for defendant ExxonMobil Oil Corp. Argued and the court took the matter under submission. The court ruled on plaintiff's objections as follows: objection number 16 is sustained, overruled as to the remainder. Defendant's objections are sustained as to numbers 1, 3 and 4; number 5 is overruled in part; remainder of the objections are overruled. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR 581 --- After hearing the court ruled as follows: Defendant CCSF's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is denied. As to the Res Judicata/Collateral Esptoppel arguments on the 4th and 5th causes of action, there was no reference in the prior action to "permanent" loss of access. The issues in the Court of Appeal decision involved the duty to inspect and repair under the Lease. By implication, a duty to repair means that the wharf must be repaired and, therefore, loss of access would not be permanent. Furthermore, the jury awarded Plaintiff $2.8 million for construction costs to rebuild the processing facility, also implying that the case was not about a permanent loss of access. As to the 4th cause of action for Breach of Contract, Plaintiff has submitted evidence that raises a triable issue of material fact as to several alleged obligations and breaches. As to the 5th cause of action for Breach of Implied Covenant of Quiet Enjoyment, Defendant has failed to show that a claim for breach of the covenant could not be established. There is plenty of evidence to establish triable issues of fact (Port named as dischargers of the pollution initially and later in time when it demolished the Wharf; the pollution has prevented Plaintiff from rebuilding its facilities.) Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order.

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF F. ALIOTO FISH COMPANY, INC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Laura H. McKaskle of Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP (213.612.2500) appearing via Court Call for defendant and responding party Exxon Mobil; James P. Walsh of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (276.6556) for plaintiff and moving party; David Ammons of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3954) for defendant and responding party City and County of San Francisco.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a 1st amended complaint is granted. However, plaintiff must file the complaint after the hearing of the motion for summary judgment. The court will hear the motion for summary judgment on August 22, 2011, but indicated it will consider an application for an order shortening time to hear a motion for summary judgment if this new cause of action is the only cause of action remaining. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
